sections.paragraph:
ὀρθόω (ὀρθός) fut. 3 sg. ὀρθώσει Sir 27:14; 1 aor. ὤρθωσα. Pass.: aor. ὠρθώθην LXX; pf. 3 sg. ὤρθωται Esth 7:9, ptc. ὠρθώμενος 2 Esdr 6:11; (Hom. et al.; LXX; PsSol 10:3; Philo) set upright σταυρόν GPt 4:11 (cp. of a mast, Lucian, Catapl. 1; ξύλον=gallows, Esth 7:9).—DELG s.v. ὀρθός.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 722
